betterquesting:10.desc:8:
Supporting circuit boards that can hold 4 electron tubes, the Electrical Stimulator provides a variety of functions. Use a Forestry Soldering Iron on an Intricate Circuit Board to set four slots worth of electron tubes. The tubes' capabilities are listed here as percentages:

Iron ET -> +0.5 Production
Diamantine ET -> +1.5 Production
Apatine ET -> 1.5x Pollination (Max 5x)
Obsidian ET -> 0.8x Lifespan
Lapis ET -> 1.5x Lifespan (Max 5x)
Blazing ET -> Simulates Hellish temperature
Golden ET -> 1.5x Mutation Rate (Max 5x)
Tin ET -> 0.4x Territory (Min 0.1) & -0.1 Production
Bronze ET -> 1.5x Territory (Max 5x)

Genetic Decay is also increased by 50% for all of them. This affects how long until ignoble bees die off, so don't worry about it if your bees are pristine.

Remember that lifespan is represented by ticks, not time. You need a world accelerator if you want a lifespan that is really less than 1 tick.

[note]Breeder's Notes:
12x Golden ETs and 20x Obsidian ETs across eight of these will make a super breeding party alveary; it will spike your mutation chances while dramatically decreasing lifespan. If you have been using the oblivion frame in an apiary to shorten lifespan for breeding, then this *does not* replace that because you cannot use world accelerators in alvearies. So the shortest you can reduce a bee's life cycle to is 1 tick = 27 seconds. On the other hand, dramatically increasing the mutation rate in the alveary may be faster for crossbreeding.

Production Apiarist's Notes:
Alternately, you can slam down three of these with:
* 10x Diamantine
* 2x Iron
That'll take you right up to the production limit. Any higher and you risk your pristine princesses turning ignoble from overwork.[/note]
betterquesting:10.name:8: A Stim a Day Keeps the Doctor Awake
